method: manual claude -p triggering check: each query run as a real nested `claude -p` with the skill available; killed early after the first tool decisions; a run counts as TRIGGERED iff the model consults the diagram skill (Skill{diagram} or Read of skills/diagram/SKILL.md) among its first tool uses.
note_on_automated_loop: The skill-creator run_loop/run_eval harness scored recall~0 on this skill and is INVALID here: it stubs the skill as a transient slash-command (-skill-<hash>) and only counts a trigger if the model's FIRST tool call is Skill/Read of that hash. Slash-commands do not auto-trigger like skills, and diagram tasks often open with Bash (check d2) or reading the spec, so genuine triggers read as misses. Manual verification is the source of truth.
eval_set: evals/trigger-eval.json (23 queries: 13 should-trigger incl. no-spec, 10 should-not near-misses)

results.query: draw the architecture of feature FS-14711 ... clean and readable browsable page
no spec, just the code — draw how these three .cls files interact
the prompt-storage-flow diagram is hard to read, fix the layout
visualize the object model — object, fields, permset grants — as a readable page
explain in words how the sealed-base pattern works — no picture needed
